title: node-postgres Pools, Queries, Transactions, and Failure Semantics · Type-System Trace: Pg Ambiguous Commit
summary: Handlers create pools, transactions use pool.query across clients, SQL interpolates identifiers and values, clients leak on error, and ambiguous commits are blindly retried. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts pg ambiguous commit increment in bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.
Run BEGIN, all statements, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on one client with isolation, constraints, locking, retries, and domain invariant evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Pg Ambiguous Commit · Theory Pg Ambiguous Commit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5841 in a partner integration using an older generated TypeScript client. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Pg Ambiguous Commit contract for the supplied data.
